Abel Mendoza
Robotics & Embedded Systems Engineer
Anaheim, CA
test.candidate@example.com | (562) 322-2299
linkedin.com/in/test-candidate | example.dev | github.com/test-candidate
Professional Summary
Robotics and embedded systems engineer with hands-on experience deploying, repairing, validating, and integrating
autonomous robotic and industrial hardware systems in real-world environments. Skilled in ROS2, embedded Linux,
hardware validation, sensor fusion, industrial communications, and full-stack system integration across software, elec-
tronics, networking, and control layers. Proven ability to diagnose failures and restore systems under live operational
pressure. Targeting aerospace, defense, robotics, and embedded autonomy roles. U.S. citizen eligible for ITAR/EAR
work.
Technical Skills
Autonomy & Robotics: ROS2 Humble, PX4 Autopilot (SITL), MAVLink, MAVSDK, SLAM, Kalman filtering, ob-
stacle avoidance
Perception: OpenCV, ArUco tracking, Intel RealSense D455, YOLOv8 (Jetson TensorRT), depth sensing
Embedded & Electronics: Raspberry Pi 4B, NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano, STM32, PIC32, FPGA tooling, UART, SPI,
I2C, PWM, GPIO
Electrical Test & Validation: Multimeters, optical power meters, programmable electronic loads, DC-DC converter
testing, relay logic validation, continuity testing
Systems & Networking: Linux (Ubuntu 22.04), Docker, Tailscale VPN, WebSockets, TCP/UDP, distributed systems
Programming: Python, C++, Go, TypeScript, JavaScript, Bash
Tools: Git, VS Code, FastAPI, Next.js, React, CycloneDDS, MPLAB ICD3, Quartus
Professional Experience
Electronics Test & Validation Technician Apr 2026 – Present
RLH Industries, Inc. — Orange, CA
• Performed validation, calibration, and troubleshooting of fiber optic communication modules, verifying electrical →
optical → electrical signal integrity across telecom and industrial communication systems
• Measured voltage, current, resistance, and continuity using multimeters, programmable electronic loads, and bench
power supplies
• Validated optical transmission systems at 850nm and 1310nm wavelengths using calibrated optical power meters and
dBm measurements
• Executed DC-DC power converter testing and calibration including voltage regulation, load testing (∼95–100W),
tolerance verification (±0.5%), and thermal drift observation
• Calibrated relay threshold detection and alarm logic circuits (VR1/VR2), validating LED indicators, switching behav-
ior, and fault conditions
• Performed firmware flashing, embedded hardware bring-up, and web interface deployment for industrial Ethernet relay
and I/O systems using MPLAB ICD3, PIC32 microcontrollers, MPFS2, USB Blaster, and Quartus
• Conducted board-level troubleshooting and failure isolation using continuity testing, embedded network diagnostics,
and known-good reference hardware (“golden boards”)
• Assisted with FPGA programming, board-level validation, and integration testing for industrial communication and
supervisory control systems
Robotics Deployment Technician Jan 2025 – Dec 2025
Armstrong Robotics — Hollywood, CA
• Maintained 24/7 deployment of an autonomous robotic system in a high-volume production environment as the sole
on-site technical owner
• Assembled and integrated new hardware components to improve system autonomy and operational reliability

-- 1 of 2 --

• Performed repairs across mechanical, electrical, and control systems during live robotic operation
• Diagnosed and resolved failures across hardware, perception, and control systems under real-time constraints
• Replaced and calibrated Robotiq grippers on UFactory robotic arms and validated performance under production load
• Calibrated vision systems using ArUco markers and supported live iteration with engineering teams
• Acted as liaison between operators, staff, and engineers during system failures and recovery
Field Robotics Agent Mar 2025 – May 2025
Serve Robotics — Los Angeles, CA
• Supported deployment and expansion of autonomous delivery robots across multiple LA regions
• Diagnosed navigation, sensor, and connectivity issues during real-world operation and validated fixes under time
pressure
• Collaborated with engineering teams to surface edge cases and improve system reliability
Technical Projects
Omega-1 — Distributed Autonomous Robotics Platform
Raspberry Pi 4B | Jetson Orin Nano | ROS2 | FastAPI | Next.js
• Architected a distributed robotics system across multiple machines enabling real-time control, perception, and moni-
toring over a VPN-secured network
• Implemented GPS-free localization using Kalman filter sensor fusion combining odometry with ArUco-based landmark
corrections
• Built real-time obstacle avoidance system with ultrasonic sensing and dynamic motion-state recovery
• Designed low-latency video streaming pipeline using custom MJPEG proxy infrastructure
• Integrated YOLOv8 object detection on Jetson Orin with optimized compressed streaming
• Debugged PWM clock interference corrupting servo signals and resolved through timing validation and signal clamping
• Built full-stack control dashboard with React and Xbox controller teleoperation support
PX4 Autonomous Mission Stack
ROS2 | PX4 | Gazebo
• Built UAV autonomy pipeline with offboard control, navigation logic, and mission-state handling
• Implemented obstacle avoidance and telemetry visualization using ROS2 and MAVSDK
Jetson RealSense Perception Stack
Jetson Orin Nano | ROS2
• Built RGB-D perception pipeline with depth streaming, IMU integration, and obstacle detection
• Developed diagnostics to validate sensor data, frame rates, and runtime system performance
